feature_list.test_description: Verify direct support for 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation.
feature_list.test_constraints: Only the stated 100 Kbps normal-mode support is in scope; where implemented.
test_steps: Configure the core for 100 Kbps I2C operation.
Apply I2C traffic using SCL and SDA at the normal-mode rate.
Observe operation over the interface.
expected_results: The core operates correctly at 100 Kbps normal-mode.
No additional mode behavior is assumed beyond direct 100 Kbps support.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core can directly support 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation, where implemented.
feature_list.requirement_text:
The interface defines 3 transmission speeds:
- Normal: 100Kbps
Only 100Kbps and 400Kbps modes are supported directly.

feature_list.test_description: Verify direct support for 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation.
feature_list.test_constraints: Only the stated 400 Kbps fast-mode support is in scope; where correct hardware is included.
test_steps: Configure the core for 400 Kbps I2C operation.
Apply I2C traffic using SCL and SDA at the fast-mode rate.
Observe operation over the interface.
expected_results: The core operates correctly at 400 Kbps fast-mode.
No additional mode behavior is assumed beyond direct 400 Kbps support.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core can support 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation, where correct hardware is included.
feature_list.requirement_text:
The interface defines 3 transmission speeds:
- Fast: 400Kbps
Only 100Kbps and 400Kbps modes are supported directly.

feature_list.test_description: Verify the core presents a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
feature_list.test_constraints: Compliance is limited to the interface described by the listed WISHBONE signals.
test_steps: Connect to the core using the listed WISHBONE signals.
Perform a standard WISHBONE Classic access sequence.
Observe bus handshaking and interrupt/output behavior.
expected_results: The interface operates as a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
The listed WISHBONE signals are present and used for access.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core shall implement a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
feature_list.requirement_text: The core features a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.

feature_list.test_description: Verify each WISHBONE access completes in two clock cycles.
feature_list.test_constraints: Applies to a WISHBONE access using wb_clk_i, wb_cyc_i, wb_stb_i, and wb_ack_o.
test_steps: Initiate a WISHBONE access with wb_cyc_i and wb_stb_i asserted.
Count wb_clk_i cycles until wb_ack_o indicates completion.
Repeat for a read or write access.
expected_results: Each access completes after two clock cycles.
The access handshake is observable through wb_ack_o.
feature_list.requirement_description: Each WISHBONE access must take two clock cycles.

feature_list.test_description: Verify asynchronous reset can be configured as active high.
feature_list.test_constraints: Applies when ARST_LVL is set to 1'b1.
test_steps: Set ARST_LVL to active high.
Assert arst_i high.
Observe core reset behavior.
expected_results: The core treats arst_i=1 as the active reset level.
Reset is asserted when arst_i is high.
feature_list.requirement_description: The asynchronous reset level is configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level can be set to either active high (1’b1) or active low (1’b0)

feature_list.test_description: Verify asynchronous reset can be configured as active low.
feature_list.test_constraints: Applies when ARST_LVL is set to 1'b0.
test_steps: Set ARST_LVL to active low.
Assert arst_i low.
Observe core reset behavior.
expected_results: The core treats arst_i=0 as the active reset level.
Reset is asserted when arst_i is low.
feature_list.requirement_description: The asynchronous reset level is configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level can be set to either active high (1’b1) or active low (1’b0)

feature_list.test_description: Verify the core exposes SCL pad input, output, and output-enable signals.
feature_list.test_constraints: Applies to scl_pad_i, scl_pad_o, and scl_pad_oe as listed external connections.
test_steps: Connect to the SCL pad signals.
Drive and monitor the SCL-related signals.
Observe signal presence and direction behavior.
expected_results: scl_pad_i is present as an input.
scl_pad_o and scl_pad_oe are present as outputs.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core must expose SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.requirement_text:
scl_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Clock line input
scl_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output
scl_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output enable
sda_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Data line input
sda_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output
sda_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output enable

feature_list.test_description: Verify the core exposes SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals.
feature_list.test_constraints: Applies to sda_pad_i, sda_pad_o, and sda_pad_oe as listed external connections.
test_steps: Connect to the SDA pad signals.
Drive and monitor the SDA-related signals.
Observe signal presence and direction behavior.
expected_results: sda_pad_i is present as an input.
sda_pad_o and sda_pad_oe are present as outputs.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core must expose SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.requirement_text:
scl_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Clock line input
scl_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output
scl_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output enable
sda_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Data line input
sda_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output
sda_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output enable
